title: Foreign Account Tax Compliance Act
summary: FATCA is a 2010 U.S. tax compliance law requiring foreign financial institutions to report information about U.S. taxpayer accounts to the IRS. This Treasury page serves as an index of Model Intergovernmental Agreements (IGAs) and their status by jurisdiction. The content is purely informational and archival in nature, listing which countries have IGAs in force, signed, or in substance for FATCA implementation purposes.
key_points: FATCA was enacted in 2010 by Congress to target non-compliance by U.S. taxpayers using foreign accounts
Foreign financial institutions must either register directly with the IRS or comply via Intergovernmental Agreements (IGAs) with their jurisdictions
The page provides a status directory of IGAs by jurisdiction (In Force, Signed, Agreement in Substance)
Two IGA model types exist: Model 1 and Model 2, with varying implementation dates across jurisdictions
Many jurisdictions reached IGA in-force status by mid-2014 (6-30-2014 or 11-30-2014 common dates)
Singapore appears notable as it had a Model 1 superseding a previous Model 1, effective 1-1-2021
why_it_matters: This page has no direct market-moving relevance. It is an administrative reference page listing the status of tax information-sharing agreements between the U.S. and foreign jurisdictions. It does not contain policy decisions, economic data, or market signals. The information is historical/archival in nature and cannot serve as a macro catalyst for trading decisions.
